title: Fix import error for eval_logger in score utils
body:
## 🛠 Description of the Issue

Some tasks (e.g., `score` and others under `lm_eval/tasks/`) attempt to import `eval_logger` using:

```python
from lm_eval.utils import eval_logger
```

or:

```python
from lm_eval.loggers import eval_logger
```

However, `eval_logger` is no longer defined in those modules, resulting in the following runtime error:

```
ImportError: cannot import name 'eval_logger' from 'lm_eval.utils'
```

This prevents tasks from running correctly, including `score_robustness_agieval` and potentially others relying on similar logging mechanisms.

## ✅ Fix

To resolve the issue across all affected tasks, the following replacement was made:

```python
import logging
eval_logger = logging.getLogger("lm_eval.score")
```

This change restores proper logger usage by leveraging Python's standard logging infrastructure and avoids relying on deprecated or non-existent imports.

The fix has been applied consistently across all tasks that were impacted by this issue.

Explore the source code to identify the files and functions to modify.

step_plan.summary: Fixes runtime ImportErrors caused by deprecated `eval_logger` imports by defining `eval_logger` using Python’s standard logging (`logging.getLogger("lm_eval.score")`) in all affected score/metric utility modules. This preserves existing log calls without changing scoring or aggregation logic.

steps.operation: Replace deprecated eval_logger import with stdlib logger initialization (and ensure the function uses the module-level logger)
steps.reason: `process_docs_add_prompts` calls `eval_logger.error(...)` on missing template files; importing `eval_logger` from `lm_eval.utils` can raise ImportError and prevent any downstream task (e.g., math robustness) from importing this module.
steps.side_effects: Purely affects logging/import-time behavior; no change to dataset mapping logic. Verify module imports cleanly and that callers like `utils_math.prompt_robustness_process_docs` still work. May slightly change logger name/format depending on logging configuration.

steps.operation: Replace deprecated eval_logger import with stdlib logger initialization (and keep error handling unchanged)
steps.reason: This function logs an error and exits when prompt templates are missing; it must not rely on a removed `lm_eval.utils.eval_logger` symbol so tasks importing/using option-order robustness processing don’t fail at import time.
steps.side_effects: No behavioral change except logger source/name; sys.exit path remains the same. Ensure no duplicate logger definitions or import-order issues in `utils.py` after adding `import logging` and `eval_logger = logging.getLogger("lm_eval.score")`.

steps.operation: Replace deprecated eval_logger import with stdlib logger initialization (and keep error handling unchanged)
steps.reason: This function also logs on FileNotFoundError; without a valid module-level `eval_logger`, importing this module fails and breaks callers such as `utils_math.non_greedy_robustness_process_docs`.
steps.side_effects: Only changes logging initialization; data augmentation/map logic remains unchanged. Confirm that `options_format` handling and `doc.map(...)` behavior are unaffected.

steps.operation: Replace deprecated eval_logger import with stdlib logger initialization for Perspective API metric logging
steps.reason: `toxicity_perspective_api` uses `eval_logger.warning/error(...)` in multiple branches (missing env vars, unexpected API responses, retry failures). If the module imports `eval_logger` from a non-existent location, this metric cannot be imported or executed.
steps.side_effects: Logging category/name changes to `lm_eval.score` (per PR intent). Ensure any tests or tasks that assert on logger names/messages are updated if they exist. Runtime behavior of API calls and error raising remains the same.

steps.operation: Replace deprecated eval_logger import with stdlib logger initialization; keep per-prompt accuracy reporting intact
steps.reason: This aggregation helper logs informational accuracy; fixing logger initialization prevents ImportError when `utils_agieval.py` is imported by AGIEval-related tasks/utilities.
steps.side_effects: No metric logic changes; only logger binding changes. Ensure the module-level `eval_logger` exists before function use and that logging does not introduce circular imports.

steps.operation: Replace deprecated eval_logger import with stdlib logger initialization; keep per-option accuracy reporting intact
steps.reason: This function depends on `eval_logger.info(...)`; switching to `logging.getLogger("lm_eval.score")` removes dependency on deprecated imports and keeps logging calls valid.
steps.side_effects: No change to accuracy computation; only affects where logs are emitted. If log filters are configured by logger name, they may need to include `lm_eval.score`.

steps.operation: Replace deprecated eval_logger import with stdlib logger initialization; keep non-greedy accuracy reporting intact
steps.reason: Ensures `eval_logger` is defined in-module so that non-greedy aggregation functions don’t fail due to missing/removed `eval_logger` import.
steps.side_effects: No functional changes to aggregation; only logging initialization changes. Validate importability and basic execution via a lightweight unit/integration run of an AGIEval task.

steps.operation: Replace deprecated eval_logger import with stdlib logger initialization; keep category-wise prompt macro accuracy logs intact
steps.reason: This function logs per-category accuracy per prompt; resolving logger initialization prevents ImportError when MMLU-Pro utilities are imported.
steps.side_effects: No changes to macro-averaging; only logger source/name changes. Ensure any consuming code that configures loggers still captures these info logs.

steps.operation: Replace deprecated eval_logger import with stdlib logger initialization; keep category-wise option macro accuracy logs intact
steps.reason: Like other utilities, this uses `eval_logger.info(...)` and must not depend on removed `lm_eval.utils.eval_logger`/`lm_eval.loggers.eval_logger` symbols.
steps.side_effects: No changes to calculations; only logging initialization changes. Confirm that `always_opt` filtering behavior is unchanged.

steps.operation: Replace deprecated eval_logger import with stdlib logger initialization; keep non-greedy category macro accuracy logs intact
steps.reason: Prevents import-time failures in MMLU-Pro utils by defining `eval_logger` via Python logging while keeping existing informational logging statements working.
steps.side_effects: Only impacts logging. Validate that downstream result processors that import this module load successfully.

steps.operation: Replace deprecated eval_logger import with stdlib logger initialization; keep per-prompt math accuracy logging intact
steps.reason: This function logs per-prompt accuracy and will error if `eval_logger` import is broken; fixing logger initialization ensures math task utilities import successfully.
steps.side_effects: No changes to computed accuracy; only logger binding changes. Confirm no namespace collisions with other `utils` modules.

steps.operation: Replace deprecated eval_logger import with stdlib logger initialization; keep non-greedy math accuracy logging intact
steps.reason: Ensures `eval_logger.info(...)` remains valid and the module is importable; this is critical for tasks that use math non-greedy scoring/aggregation.
steps.side_effects: No changes to `math_equal` usage or scoring; only logging changes. Run a smoke test importing `utils_math` and executing a representative aggregation to ensure no NameError/ImportError.
